context:
People in this club who perform in school talent shows often attend and are very engaged with school events.
People in this club either perform in school talent shows often or are inactive and disinterested community members.
People in this club who chaperone high school dances are not students who attend the school.
All people in this club who are inactive and disinterested members of their community chaperone high school dances.
All young children and teenagers in this club who wish to further their academic careers and educational opportunities are students who attend the school. 
Bonnie is in this club and she either both attends and is very engaged with school events and is a student who attends the school or is not someone who both attends and is very engaged with school events and is not a student who attends the school.
conclusion: Bonnie performs in school talent shows often.
label: Uncertain

in_club(x) ::: x is in the club

attend_school_events(x) ::: x attends and is very engaged with school events
inactive_disinterested(x) ::: x is inactive and disinterested
chaperone_dance(x) ::: x chaperones high school dances
student(x) ::: x is a student who attends the school
young_child_teenager(x) ::: x is a young child or teenager
wish_further_education(x) ::: x wishes to further their academic careers and educational opportunities

all x (in_club(x) -> (inactive_disinterested(x) -> chaperone_dance(x)))
all x (in_club(x) & young_child_teenager(x) & wish_further_education(x) -> student(x))

CONCLUSION:
perform_talent_show(bonnie)

context:
All employees who schedule a meeting with their customers will go to the company building today. 
Everyone who has lunch in the company building schedules meetings with their customers. 
Employees have lunch either in the company building or at home.
If an employee has lunch at home, they are working remotely from home.
All employees who are in other countries work remotely from home. 
No managers work remotely from home. 
James will appear in the company today if and only if he is a manager.
conclusion: James has lunch in the company.
label: Uncertain
formalisations:
PREDICATES:
employee(x) ::: x is an employee
schedule_meeting_with_customers(x) ::: x schedules a meeting with their customers
go_to_company_building(x) ::: x goes to the company building today
have_lunch_in_company_building(x) ::: x has lunch in the company building
have_lunch_at_home(x) ::: x has lunch at home
work_remotely_from_home(x) ::: x works remotely from home
in_other_countries(x) ::: x is in other countries
manager(x) ::: x is a manager

PREMISES:
all x (schedule_meeting_with_customers(x) -> go_to_company_building(x))
all x (have_lunch_in_company_building(x) -> schedule_meeting_with_customers(x))
all x (employee(x) -> (have_lunch_in_company_building(x) | have_lunch_at_home(x)))
all x (have_lunch_at_home(x) -> work_remotely_from_home(x))
all x (employee(x) & in_other_countries(x) -> work_remotely_from_home(x))
-manager(x) -> -work_remotely_from_home(x)
all x (manager(x) <-> (go_to_company_building(x)))
CONCLUSION:
have_lunch_in_company_building(james)

When the Monkeypox virus occurs in a being, it may get Monkeypox. 
Monkeypox virus can occur in certain animals.
Humans are mammals.
Mammals are animals.
Symptoms of Monkeypox include fever, headache, muscle pains, and tiredness. 
People feel tired when they get the flu.
conclusion: There is an animal.

monkeypox(x) ::: x has monkeypox

human(x) ::: x is a human
has_fever(x) ::: x has a fever
has_headache(x) ::: x has a headache
has_muscle_pains(x) ::: x has muscle pains
is_tired(x) ::: x is tired

all x (has_flu(x) -> is_tired(x))

CONCLUSION:
animal(x)

Romance languages are a language family.
All languages within a language family are related to each other.

German is related to Spanish.
Basque is not related to any other language.
conclusion: Basque is a Romance language.

related_to(x, y) ::: x is related to y

all x (language_family(x) -> all y (member_of(y, x) -> related_to(y, x)))

romance_language(spanish)
related_to(german, spanish)
-related_to(basque, x)

Some restaurants that do not provide take-out service receive many negative reviews.
All restaurants that are popular among local residents have ratings greater than four.
The Hamden Plaza Subway store has a rating greater than four, or it is popular among local residents.
conclusion: If the Hamden Plaza Subway store provides take-out service and receives many negative reviews, then its rating is greater than 4 and it does not provide take-out service.

formalisations:

yelp_recommendation(x) ::: x is listed in Yelp’s recommendations
many_negative_reviews(x) ::: x receives many negative reviews
rating_greater_than_four(x) ::: x has a rating greater than four
take_out_service(x) ::: x provides take-out service
popular_among_local_residents(x) ::: x is popular among local residents

(rating_greater_than_four(hamden_plaza_subway) | popular_among_local_residents(hamden_plaza_subway))

CONCLUSION:
-(take_out_service(hamden_plaza_subway) & many_negative_reviews(hamden_plaza_subway))

context:
In superhero movies, the good guys always win.
The Surprising Adventures of Sir Digby Chicken Caesar is a superhero movie.
Good guys fight bad guys and vice versa.
Sir Digby fights his nemesis.
If a superhero movie is named after a character, that character is a good guy.
The Surprising Adventures of Sir Digby Chicken Caesar is named after Sir Digby.
If somebody wins a fight, the person they are fighting does not win.
If a superhero movie is named after a character, that character is in the movie.
conclusion: Sir Digby’s nemesis does not win.
label: True
formalisations:
PREDICATES:
superhero_movie(x) ::: x is a superhero movie
good_guy(x) ::: x is a good guy
bad_guy(x) ::: x is a bad guy
fight(x,y) ::: x fights y
named_after(x,y) ::: x is named after y
wins(x,y) ::: x wins against y

PREMISES:
superhero_movie(the_surprising_adventures_of_sir_digby_chicken_caesar)
good_guy(x) | bad_guy(x)
fight(x,y) |-fight(y,x)
all x (superhero_movie(x) -> exists y (named_after(x,y)))
all x (named_after(x,y) -> good_guy(y))
superhero_movie(the_surprising_adventures_of_sir_digby_chicken_caesar)
named_after(the_surprising_adventures_of_sir_digby_chicken_caesar,sir_digby)
wins(x,y) |-wins(y,x)
all x (superhero_movie(x) -> exists y (in_movie(y,x)))
fight(sir_digby,nemesis(sir_digby))

CONCLUSION:
-wins(nemesis(sir_digby),sir_digby)

superhero_movie(x) ::: x is a superhero movie
good_guy(x) ::: x is a good guy
bad_guy(x) ::: x is a bad guy

wins(x,y) ::: x wins against y

PREMISES:

named_after(the_surprising_adventures_of_sir_digby_chicken_caesar,sir_digby)
wins(x,y) |-wins(y,x)
all x (superhero_movie(x) -> exists y (in_movie(y,x)))

A natural language processing task can be categorized as either a language generation task or a language understanding task.
If the output of a natural language processing task is a text sequence, then it is a language generation task.
Machine translation is a natural language processing task.
The output of machine translation is a text sequence.
conclusion: Machine translation is a language generation task.
label: True
formalisations:
PREDICATES:
natural_language_processing_task(x) ::: x is a natural language processing task
language_generation_task(x) ::: x is a language generation task
language_understanding_task(x) ::: x is a language understanding task
text_sequence(x) ::: x is a text sequence

PREMISES:
all x (natural_language_processing_task(x) -> (language_generation_task(x) | language_understanding_task(x)))
all x (text_sequence(x) -> language_generation_task(x))

text_sequence(machine_translation)

CONCLUSION:
language_generation_task(machine_translation)

context:
Pets are allowed in some managed buildings.
A deposit is required to rent an apartment in a managed building.
The security deposit can be either equal to the monthly rent at a managed building or more.
Fluffy is Tom's cat.
Cats are pets.
The Olive Garden is a managed building.
The monthly rent at the Olive Garden is $2000.
$2000 is more than $1500.
Tom will rent an apartment in a managed building if he is allowed to move in with Fluffy, and the security deposit is no more than $1500.
If a managed building allows pets, then people are allowed to move in with a pet.
conclusion: Tom will rent an apartment in The Olive Garden.
label: False
formalisations:
PREDICATES:

managed_building(x) ::: x is a managed building
deposit(x,y) ::: the security deposit for x is y
rent(x,y) ::: the monthly rent for x is y
cat(x) ::: x is a cat
owns(x,y) ::: x owns y
person(x) ::: x is a person

PREMISES:
pets_allowed(olive_garden)
deposit(olive_garden,y)
rent(olive_garden,2000)
2000 > 1500
cat(fluffy)
fluffy = tom's_cat
cats_are_pets
managed_building(olive_garden)
all x (managed_building(x) -> deposit(x,y))
all x (deposit(x,y) | y = rent(x,z) | y > rent(x,z))
all x (pets_allowed(x) -> exists y (person(y) & owns(y,z) & cat(z)))
all x (managed_building(x) -> exists y (person(y) & owns(y,z) & cat(z)))
exists x (managed_building(x) & pets_allowed(x) & deposit(x,y) & y <= 1500) -> tom_rents(olive_garden)

CONCLUSION:
tom_rents(olive_garden)

context:
If people spill a lot of food on their clothing, then they are not notably tidy.
All clumsy foodies who go out frequently to find new food restaurants to try are people who spill a lot of food on their clothing.
If people are cleanly, then they are notably tidy.
If people value order and spotlessness, then they are cleanly.
All people whose families prioritize order and spotlessness themselves value order and spotlessness.
Peter is either a person who spills a lot of food on his clothing and is cleanly, or a person who does not spill a lot of food on his clothing and is not cleanly
conclusion: Peter is notably tidy.
label: Uncertain
formalisations:
PREDICATES:
spill_a_lot_of_food_on_clothing(x) ::: x spills a lot of food on their clothing
not_notably_tidy(x) ::: x is not notably tidy
clumsy_foodie(x) ::: x is a clumsy foodie
go_out_frequently_to_find_new_food_restaurants_to_try(x) ::: x goes out frequently to find new food restaurants to try
person(x) ::: x is a person
cleanly(x) ::: x is cleanly
value_order_and_spotlessness(x) ::: x values order and spotlessness
prioritize_order_and_spotlessness(x) ::: x's family prioritizes order and spotlessness

PREMISES:
all x (spill_a_lot_of_food_on_clothing(x) -> -not_notably_tidy(x))
all x (clumsy_foodie(x) & go_out_frequently_to_find_new_food_restaurants_to_try(x) -> person(x) & spill_a_lot_of_food_on_clothing(x))
all x (cleanly(x) -> not_notably_tidy(x))
all x (value_order_and_spotlessness(x) -> cleanly(x))
all x (prioritize_order_and_spotlessness(x) -> value_order_and_spotlessness(x))
person(peter)
(spill_a_lot_of_food_on_clothing(peter) & cleanly(peter)) | (-spill_a_lot_of_food_on_clothing(peter) & -cleanly(peter))

CONCLUSION:
not_notably_tidy(peter)

context:
If people spill a lot of food on their clothing, then they are not notably tidy.
All clumsy foodies who go out frequently to find new food restaurants to try are people who spill a lot of food on their clothing.
If people are cleanly, then they are notably tidy.
If people value order and spotlessness, then they are cleanly.
All people whose families prioritize order and spotlessness themselves value order and spotlessness.
Peter is either a person who spills a lot of food on his clothing and is cleanly, or a person who does not spill a lot of food on his clothing and is not cleanly
conclusion: Peter is either both a clumsy foodie who goes out frequently to find new food restaurants to try and issomeone whose family prioritizes order and spotlessness, or he neither is a clumsy foodie who goes out frequently to find new food restaurants to try nor does his family prioritize order and spotlessness.

All of Peter's pets that are mammals are also animals.
All monkeys are mammals. 
Peter's pets are all either monkeys or birds. 
Peter's birds can fly.
All animals that are Peter's pets can breathe.

Rock is Peter's pet.
Rock can fly, or Rock is a bird, or Rock cannot breathe.
conclusion: Rock is a monkey.

animal(x) ::: x is an animal
monkey(x) ::: x is a monkey
bird(x) ::: x is a bird
fly(x) ::: x can fly
wings(x) ::: x has wings
breathe(x) ::: x can breathe

all x (animal(x) & pet(x) -> breathe(x))

context:
All people who make wedding plans are people who are engaged.
All people who invite others to come to their ceremony make wedding plans.
Anyone who has a well-attended wedding invites others to come to their ceremony.
Some people that have well-attended weddings have larger families.
If John is engaged, then John does not invite friends to their ceremony and make wedding plans.
If John has a larger family, then either John has a well-attended wedding or invites friends to their ceremony.
conclusion: John has a larger family.
label: False
formalisations:
PREDICATES:
make_wedding_plans(x) ::: x makes wedding plans
engaged(x) ::: x is engaged
invite_others(x) ::: x invites others to come to their ceremony
has_well_attended_wedding(x) ::: x has a well-attended wedding
has_larger_family(x) ::: x has a larger family

PREMISES:
all x (make_wedding_plans(x) -> engaged(x))
all x (invite_others(x) -> make_wedding_plans(x))
all x (has_well_attended_wedding(x) -> invite_others(x))
exists x (has_well_attended_wedding(x) & has_larger_family(x))
all x (engaged(x) -> -invite_others(x) & -make_wedding_plans(x))
all x (has_larger_family(x) -> (has_well_attended_wedding(x) | invite_others(x)))

CONCLUSION:
has_larger_family(john)

context:
Machine Learning algorithms can be categorized as supervised learning, unsupervised learning, and reinforcement learning.
Unsupervised learning algorithms do not require labeled data.
The state-of-the-art text summarization model is trained with machine learning algorithms.
Reinforcement learning is not used to train the state-of-the-art text summarization model.
The Machine Learning algorithm for training text summarization models requires labeled data.
conclusion: Supervised learning is used to train the state-of-the-art text summarization model.
label: True
formalisations:
PREDICATES:
machine_learning_algorithm(x) ::: x is a machine learning algorithm
supervised_learning(x) ::: x is supervised learning
unsupervised_learning(x) ::: x is unsupervised learning
reinforcement_learning(x) ::: x is reinforcement learning
labeled_data(x) ::: x requires labeled data
text_summarization_model(x) ::: x is the state-of-the-art text summarization model

PREMISES:
all x (machine_learning_algorithm(x) | supervised_learning(x) | unsupervised_learning(x) | reinforcement_learning(x))
-reinforcement_learning(text_summarization_model)
labeled_data(text_summarization_model)
text_summarization_model(text_summarization_model)
all x (supervised_learning(x) -> labeled_data(x))

CONCLUSION:
supervised_learning(text_summarization_model)
